ogTitle: Bluff Point vs Blythewood: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bluff Point and Blythewood.
heading: Bluff Point vs Blythewood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bluff Point, Western Australia and Blythewood, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bluff Point has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Blythewood (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bluff Point or Blythewood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bluff Point has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Blythewood scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bluff Point scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Blythewood scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bluff Point or Blythewood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bluff Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
